title: Templates and Jinja2
content:
### Rendering Templates Use `render_template()` to serve HTML files from the `templates/` folder: ```python from flask import Flask, render_template app = Flask(__name__) @app.route("/") def home(): return render_template("index.html", title="Home", name="Alice") ``` ### Jinja2 Template Syntax Jinja2 uses `{{ }}` for expressions and `{% %}` for control flow: ```html

{{ title }}

Hello, {{ name }}!

{{ name|upper }}

{{ price|round(2) }}

    {% for item in items %}
  • {{ item.name }} - ${{ item.price }}
    • {% endfor %}
{% if user.is_admin %} Admin Panel {% endif %} ``` ### Template Inheritance Create a base template with blocks that child templates can override: **`templates/base.html`:** ```html {% block title %}My App{% endblock %}
{% block header %}Default Header{% endblock %}
{% block content %}{% endblock %}
{% block footer %}© 2024{% endblock %}
``` **`templates/about.html`:** ```html {% extends "base.html" %} {% block title %}About Us{% endblock %} {% block content %}

About Page

This is the about page content.

{% endblock %} ``` ### Built-in Filters | Filter | Description | Example | |--------|-------------|--------| | `safe` | Marks text as safe HTML | `{{ html_content|safe }}` | | `capitalize` | Capitalizes first letter | `{{ "hello"|capitalize }}` | | `length` | Returns length | `{{ items|length }}` | | `default` | Default value if None | `{{ name|default("Guest") }}` | | `date` | Format datetime | `{{ post.date|date("Y-m-d") }}` | ### Variables Variables can be any Python type passed to `render_template`: - Strings, numbers, lists, dictionaries - Objects with attributes - Functions (callable in templates)

quiz.question: Which Jinja2 syntax is used for expressions that output values?
What keyword allows a child template to inherit from a base template?
